column2: A Windows {{c1::domain}} provides centralized authentication and device access, while a workgroup uses standalone peer systems.
column3: Domains are the business-network model; workgroups are the peer-style model.
column4: This cloze supports fast recall of the domain vs. workgroup contrast.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 6
column9: A+::220-1202::1.3 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::MicrosoftWindowsEditions Cloze Windows Domains Source::Messer-v140

column2: The command name for the Local Group Policy Editor is {{c1::gpedit.msc}}.
column3: Local Group Policy manages policy settings on a local Windows device.
column4: Objective 1.3 names gpedit.msc, so command recognition is appropriate.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.3; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 6
